commit.message:
Fix npm detection: use exit code instead of output string

The previous logic checked if "npm" appeared in output, but error messages
like "which: npm: Command not found" also contain "npm", causing false
positives. Now uses the exit code from `which` directly.

commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Remove -lbnetapi from Makefile
- Update README.md links
- Remove net API docs from memory
- The program doesn't use any network functionality
- Keep -ltracker for BFilePanel

commit.message:
Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add README with architecture overview, setup, and API docs

commit.message:
Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix injury data: add team_id + player_name to API response, fix frontend field refs

Root cause: injuries API only returned 'team' (string) and 'player' (string),
but frontend filtered by team_id and read player_name — both undefined.

Backend fix (injuries.py):
- Add _TEAM_ID_MAP: team name keyword → NBA team_id for all 30 teams
- _enrich(): adds team_id and player_name to every injury row before returning
- Applied to both live and cached responses

Frontend fix:
- Use Number() coercion on both sides of team_id comparison
- Read p.player_name || p.player || p.name for player display
- Read p.injury || p.reason for injury description
- Fix box score injMap to use correct field order

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(inheritance-tax-docs): as const配列のTypeScriptエラー修正（本番ビルド対応）

cleanupDocStateのジェネリック型推論を各setter個別呼び出しに変更。

Also adds diagnostic output when both utilization values are missing.

commit.message:
fix: change Worktree.status from String to WorktreeStatus enum (#354)

* fix: change Worktree.status from String to WorktreeStatus enum

Implement WorktreeStatus enum with variants Active, Merged, Abandoned.
Add Display and FromStr implementations, then use the impl_sql_enum!
macro to generate ToSql/FromSql for type-safe database serialization.

Update Worktree.status field type from String to WorktreeStatus, making
all status comparisons type-safe via pattern matching instead of string
literals. Update all code paths:

- is_active() now compares against WorktreeStatus::Active enum variant
- create() sets status to WorktreeStatus::Active
- delete_internal() uses WorktreeStatus::Merged or Abandoned
- update_status() signature accepts WorktreeStatus parameter
- map_worktree_row() uses typed deserialization via FromSql
- TUI rendering matches on enum variants instead of .as_str()

Eliminates stringly-typed comparisons throughout the codebase, with
full type safety from database read to display layer.

Fixes #352.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add FromStr unit tests for WorktreeStatus enum

Adds tests for all three valid variants and the invalid-input error path
to satisfy the test-coverage review request on PR #354.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Replace projectile project listing with registered repos

Add workset-repos defcustom for explicitly tracking repos, with
workset-add-repo and workset-remove-repo commands (persisted via
customize-save-variable). The *workset* listing now iterates
workset-repos instead of projectile-known-projects, showing each
repo's git worktrees. Repos with no worktrees still appear as
group headers. Binds a/R in both prefix map and list-mode keymap,
and adds a Repos group to the transient menu.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Pass 5 audit remediation — medium/low findings

- NEW-001: Atomic pertes1/2 += in combat.php war declarations (race fix)
- NEW-003: Fix Dispersée overkill condition >= 0 → > 0 (last class logic)
- NEW-TX-001: Wrap pact-break DELETE+INSERT in withTransaction() in allianceadmin.php
- NEW-TX-002: Wrap war-end UPDATE+INSERT in withTransaction() in allianceadmin.php
- NEW-002: FOR UPDATE on countStoredCompounds() to serialize concurrent synthesis
- ECON-NEW-001: Cap buy/sell quantity at 10M before intval() (float-overflow-INF guard)
- ECON-NEW-002/ECON-001: Fix MARKET_POINTS_MAX bind type 'ds'→'is' (both LEAST calls)
- MIG-M-001/002/003/004: Add IF NOT EXISTS to ADD INDEX/COLUMN in migrations
  0002, 0036, 0039, 0042 for idempotency on re-run

583 tests / 3372 assertions pass.

commit.message:
Simplify ServiceClient — remove Raw/ServiceResponse split, unify to single API

ServiceClient now has 4 generic reactive methods: postReactive, getReactive,
putReactive, deleteReactive. The caller's ParameterizedTypeReference determines
the response type — no separate Raw vs ServiceResponse method families needed.

- Delete all *Raw* and *ServiceResponse* method variants (12 methods → 4)
- Delete all *NonReactive* wrappers — callers .block() at the call site
- Merge handleErrorServiceResponse + handleErrorRaw into single handleError
- Update all callers: auth-service, web-service, tests

FASE 5: Add student progress dashboard and grade pages

## New Reusable Components (StudentProgress folder)
- RiskIndicator.razor: Visual risk level indicator (Excelente/En regla/Advertencia/Crítico)
- PerformanceCard.razor: Course performance summary card with metrics
- ProgressTrendChart.razor: Radzen line chart showing grade trends
- UpcomingTaskAlert.razor: Alert banner with upcoming task list

## New Student Pages
- MiProgreso.razor: Student progress dashboard
  - Course-by-course performance overview with risk indicators
  - Trend chart showing historical grade progression
  - Upcoming tasks sidebar for quick access
  - Overall period average and course summaries

- MisCalificaciones.razor: Grade report card/boletín
  - Course-wise grade breakdown
  - Component breakdown (tareas, quizzes, exams, projects)
  - Weighted contribution calculation
  - Final course grade with color-coded status

- TareasProximas.razor: Upcoming tasks prioritized by urgency
  - Tasks sorted by due date
  - Visual urgency indicators (4 levels)
  - Time remaining calculations
  - Direct submission links

## Navigation Updates
- Updated MainLayout.razor student navigation with 3 new routes:
  - /mi-progreso (Progress Dashboard)
  - /mis-calificaciones (Grades Report)
  - /tareas-proximas (Upcoming Tasks)
- Maintained existing student routes
- Clean integration into student workflow

FASE 5 Complete! Student UI module fully integrated with progress tracking, grade visibility, and task management.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Overhaul Lucky scanner: remove AI selection bias + expand ticker pools

Core fix - Remove AI from ticker selection:
- Autonomous scan now uses pure cryptographic random selection
- AI no longer "picks the best value play" from a subset (eliminated
  training data familiarity bias toward AAPL, MSFT, etc.)
- AI still used for predict mode discovery (needs web search for catalysts)
- The AI's job is now purely analysis, not selection

Finnhub pre-screen before expensive analysis:
- Skip stocks with price < $1.00 (penny stocks, near-delisted)
- Skip stocks with no Finnhub data (delisted/dead tickers)
- Skip on Finnhub errors (saves Gemini API calls)
- Pre-screen is cheap (1 Finnhub call) vs full analysis (5+ Gemini calls)

Ticker pool expansion and cleanup:
- Removed bankrupt/delisted: FSR, RIDE, GOEV, ARVL, VLD, ASTR, VLDR, MULN,
  NKLA, WKHS, WAY, SPCE, STEM, ALLO, CPTN
- Expanded MEGA from 50 to 84 tickers (added semis, industrials, energy)
- Expanded LARGE from 51 to 86 tickers (added SaaS, energy, international)
- Expanded MID from 50 to 82 tickers (added tech hardware, fintech, retail)
- Expanded SMALL from 48 to 82 tickers (added biotech, energy, consumer)
- Expanded MICRO from 50 to 71 tickers (added space, crypto miners, tech)
- Total pool: ~250 → ~405 tickers across all cap ranges

commit.message:
Per-segment particle deflection for detailed wind interaction

Particles now deflect against actual stroke line segments, not bounding
circles. Each frame, all strokes are transformed to world-space segments
with normals. Broad phase checks body bounding radius, narrow phase finds
closest segment to each particle.

Within 25px of a segment surface:
- Wind component into the surface gets reflected outward
- Strength proportional to distance (1.0 at surface, 0 at 25px)
- Under 8px: boundary layer turbulence noise
- Bernoulli acceleration around edges (tangential speedup)

This gives detailed flow: wind wrapping around the head specifically,
splitting between arms and body, flowing along limbs. Each surface has
its own local wind interaction.

Also: cleaner stick figure (smaller 8px head with 32 points, proper
proportions — neck, shoulders, angled arms, longer torso).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(compiler): ✨ add indentation-aware lexer for Dao (#65)

## Summary

Implements Task 1 from the implementation plan: a complete
indentation-aware lexer that translates `spec/grammar/dao.lex` into a
working token stream, with source diagnostics infrastructure and a `daoc
lex` subcommand.

## Highlights

- **Token types**: Fine-grained `TokenKind` enum covering all 17
keywords, 21 operators/punctuation, 3 literal types, identifier, and 4
synthetic tokens (INDENT, DEDENT, NEWLINE, EOF) — maps trivially to the
semantic token taxonomy in `CONTRACT_LANGUAGE_TOOLING.md`
- **Indentation handling**: Python-style indent stack with INDENT/DEDENT
emission, paren/bracket depth suppression, tab rejection with
diagnostics, and blank line skipping
- **Diagnostics infrastructure**: `SourceBuffer` (filename, contents,
line/column mapping), `Span`, `Diagnostic` with severity — shared
foundation for parser and later phases
- **Driver**: `daoc lex <file>` prints the full token stream with
line:col positions
- **Tests**: 15 tests with 84 assertions covering keywords, operators,
literals, identifiers, indentation (simple, nested, blank lines, tabs,
parens), span accuracy, and all 8 `.dao` files (4 examples + 4 syntax
probes)
- **CI**: Added ctest step to the build pipeline

## Test plan

- [ ] `cmake --build build/debug` succeeds
- [ ] `ctest --test-dir build/debug --output-on-failure` — 1/1 test
passes (15 subtests, 84 assertions)
- [ ] `daoc lex examples/hello.dao` produces correct token stream
- [ ] `daoc lex` on all 8 `.dao` files exits cleanly with balanced
INDENT/DEDENT
- [ ] `clang-format --dry-run --Werror` passes on all source
- [ ] `clang-tidy -p build/debug` reports 0 user-code warnings

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix broken hero images by removing invalid srcSet paths

The HeroImageShader was generating srcSet entries for responsive
image variants (e.g. img-about-768w.webp) that don't exist on disk.
Browsers picked the srcSet entry over src, got a 404, and showed a
broken image icon. Removed the srcSet generation since the SSR image
already handles display correctly.
